title: Animals Move Into New Wetland Home
summary: A new wetland has been built at the Great Fen nature reserve in Cambridgeshire. It took one year to make and is as big as 60 football fields. The wetland has water, channels, and special plants called reedbeds. Birds like lapwings are already coming to look for food there. The project is part of a bigger plan to connect two old wetlands. The work is paid for by the National Lottery Heritage Fund. The wetland will be open to people in the summer. The project manager, Lorna Parker, is happy to see nature take over. Rain has helped fill the water areas. Muddy edges are good for insects and small animals, which birds can eat. This place will let people see nature returning after more than 100 years.

keywords.explanation: a place where the ground is wet, like a marsh or swamp
a protected area for plants and animals
a type of bird that lives near water
an area where tall grass-like plants grow in water

questions.question: How long did it take to build the new wetland?
How big is the new wetland?
Which bird has been seen at the new wetland?
What is the name of the nature reserve where the wetland was built?
What helped fill the new water areas?
When will the wetland be open to people?

background_read: A wetland is a place where the ground is covered with water for part of the year. Wetlands are important because they provide homes for many animals and plants. They also help clean water and protect against floods. The Great Fen is a large area of wetland in Cambridgeshire, England. It is being restored to bring back wildlife that used to live there long ago.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Great Fen project team and the Wildlife Trust built the wetland.
WHAT: A new wetland area was created for wildlife.
WHERE: At the Great Fen nature reserve near Ramsey in Cambridgeshire.
WHY: To connect two ancient wetlands and bring back nature.

perspectives.description: The wetland is good for nature because it gives animals a place to live and eat.
People will be able to visit the wetland in summer and enjoy seeing nature.
